Notice Of Moton For Application To Recover Attorneys Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Feb-24-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF STEFANO PRIETO, STEFANO PRIETO Notice Of Moton For Application To Recover Attorneys Fees And Costs

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF STEFANO PRIETO'S Moton For Application To Recover Attorneys Fees And Costs

The motion is granted in part.

The parties agree fees and costs are in general awardable. The rate structure at plaintiff's firm is reasonable, i.e. $575 for the senior partner, $425 for a more junior lawyer, and $90 for the legal assistant.
The hours expended appear reasonable with these exceptions: 2.4 hours to re-do a mistaken complaint to change the date is unreasonable, and I will deduct that time (2.4 x 575). It is unreasonable to charge defendant for time to prepare for and attend an OSC which was caused by plaintiff's delay. For this I deduct 2.8 hours (2.8 x 475).
The rates are reasonable for the work. That is, while I note that some of the work may not require the skill of a higher rate lawyer, such work may be efficient-and not need further senior partner review, for example-even when done by the senior lawyer.
I address the multiplier. A positive multiplier is appropriate for the contingency, and 1.2 is generally reasonable. I reduce this to 1.1 because (a) few hours were spent on the case, showing a minimal economic impact of the contingency, (b) no other work was turned down, and (c) as a result of the limited success. I decline a further downward adjustment due to the simplicity of the work, because that is factored into the rate structure.
I agree the costs should be reduced to $847.79 because the other costs should not be recovered.

Order is signed.

Judge: Curtis E.A. Karnow, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/CK)
